key_insight: A transformer-based flow matching model can generate high-resolution path-dependent plastic stress fields in one step by treating the full loading history as a video in latent space.
engineering_relevance: This offers a fast surrogate for finite element analysis of complex geometries and history-dependent constitutive response, enabling much cheaper simulation workflows.
method: VAE latent-space generation of full stress-field trajectories
Transformer flow matching with a non-Gaussian source distribution to reduce path crossings
Token-level loading embeddings plus two auxiliary networks for better path-dependent conditioning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate high-resolution generation with limited training data and a speedup of 6–7× on CPUs and about two orders of magnitude on consumer GPUs versus FEM.
watch_item: Performance depends on representative training paths and geometries, so extrapolation to unseen loading histories or constitutive regimes may be unreliable.
paper_title: One-Step Flow Matching for Generative Modeling of Path-Dependent Physical Fields
